# `btpc`

<!-- Generated by `scripts/generate-cli-reference.sh`; do not edit. -->

BitTorrent metainfo toolkit

See [Configuration and global flags](../configuration.md) and [Exit codes and streams](../index.md#exit-codes-and-streams).

## Synopsis

```text
Usage: btpc [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>
```

## Subcommands

| Command | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [`btpc create`](create.md) | Create canonical v1, v2, or hybrid metainfo from a file or directory |

| [`btpc inspect`](inspect.md) | Inspect validated metainfo without reading payload files |

| [`btpc validate`](validate.md) | Validate metainfo structure without reading payload files |

| [`btpc verify`](verify.md) | Verify payload files against metainfo hashes |

| [`btpc edit`](edit.md) | Edit metainfo metadata without reading payload files |

| [`btpc magnet`](magnet.md) | Print a deterministic magnet URI |

| [`btpc config`](config.md) | Locate, inspect, validate, and update configuration |

| [`btpc completion`](completion.md) | Generate, install, or uninstall shell completions |

| [`btpc manpage`](manpage.md) | Generate the btpc(1) manual page on stdout |


## Deprecated aliases

- `btpc completions` — Deprecated alias for `btpc completion generate`


## Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `-h, --help` | Print help |
| `-V, --version` | Print version |

## Global options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--config, <PATH>` | Use this configuration file when configuration loading is enabled |
| `--no-config` | Disable implicit and environment-selected configuration Default: `false`. |
| `--color, <COLOR>` | Control colored terminal output Values: `auto`, `always`, `never`. |
| `-v, --verbose` | Increase diagnostic verbosity; may be repeated Default: `0`. |
| `-q, --quiet` | Suppress human summaries, warnings, and progress Default: `false`. |

## Example

```console
$ btpc --help
```
